Name: Caterpie
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

Name: Metapod
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

Name: Scyther
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Blinding Scythe attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.

Name: Shuckle
Effects: Shuffle up to 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

Name: Heracross
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It roams through forests searching for sweet nectar. Although it boasts fantastic physical strength, it’s not that good at flying.

Name: Lotad
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

Name: Lombre
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is nocturnal and becomes active at nightfall. It feeds on aquatic mosses that grow in the riverbed.

Name: Ludicolo
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Prize card you have taken.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo’s cells, making it more powerful.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in ponds and marshes that feature lots of plant life. It often fights with Dewpider, whose habitat and diet are similar.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Energy can’t be attached from your opponent’s hand to the Defending Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its thin, winglike antennae are highly absorbent. It waits out rainy days in tree hollows.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: It lives on snowy mountains. It sinks its legs into the snow to absorb water and keep its own temperature down.

Name: Abomasnow
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.

Name: Phantump
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit’s rebirth as this Pokémon.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

Name: Grubbin
Flavor-Text: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

Name: Rillaboom V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [G] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rillaboom VMAX
Effects: You may discard up to 3 [G] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Eldegoss V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Applin
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

Name: Applin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

Name: Flapple
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

Name: Appletun
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 90 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

Name: Ninetales V
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Burning Scarf attached, it gets +100 HP.
Flavor-Text: The sight of it running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night has captivated many people.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

Name: Magmortar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Each player discards the top card of their deck. This attack does 100 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Magmortar takes down its enemies by shooting fireballs, which burn them to a blackened crisp. It avoids this method when hunting prey.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

Name: Lampent
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.
Effects: Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it’s feared as an emissary of death.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have Energy attached have no Weakness.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

Name: Cinderace V
Ability-Effect: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace VMAX
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does that much more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard any Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

Name: Wingull
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest on sheer cliffs. Riding the sea breeze, it glides up into the expansive skies.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

Name: Milotic V
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tympole
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Graceful ripples running across the water’s surface are a sure sign that Tympole are singing in high-pitched voices below.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It weakens its prey with sound waves intense enough to cause headaches, then entangles them with its sticky tongue.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The vibrating of the bumps all over its body causes earthquake-like tremors. Seismitoad and Croagunk are similar species.

Name: Galarian Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.

Name: Galarian Darmanitan
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Crushing Headbutt.
Flavor-Text: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

Name: Inteleon V
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent reveals their hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Inteleon VMAX
Effects: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

Name: Arrokuda
Flavor-Text: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It’s very proud of that jaw.

Name: Barraskewda
Effects: Discard 2 [W] Energy cards from your hand. If you don’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a jaw that’s as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda’s flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

Name: Eiscue
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 60 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

Name: Eiscue V
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, heal 30 damage from it.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

Name: Electabuzz
Flavor-Text: While it’s often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.

Name: Electivire
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.

Name: Shinx
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Luxio during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

Name: Charjabug
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

Name: Boltund V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon sounds as if it’s strumming a guitar, it’s actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

Name: Toxtricity V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxtricity VMAX
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pincurchin V
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if it is Knocked Out), flip 3 coins. For each heads, put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t use that attack.
This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon on top of their deck.
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Natu
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

Name: Xatu
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to their Active Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

Name: Galarian Corsola
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

Name: Galarian Cursola
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

Name: Sigilyph
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

Name: Sandygast
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you build sand mounds when you’re playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.

Name: Palossand
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck. If this Pokémon has a Cursed Shovel attached, discard 2 more cards from the top of your opponent’s deck.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.

Name: Hatenna
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

Name: Hattrem
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

Name: Hatterene
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Each player discards a card from their hand. (Your opponent discards first.)
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

Name: Milcery
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

Name: Alcremie
Effects: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These intelligent Pokémon touch horns with each other to share information between them.

Name: Dreepy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

Name: Drakloak
Effects: Search your deck for a Dreepy and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

Name: Dragapult
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When it isn’t battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

Name: Dragapult V
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragapult VMAX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Put 5 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Farfetch’d
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The Farfetch’d of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.

Name: Galarian Sirfetch’d
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

Name: Medicham
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Barboach
Flavor-Text: Its slimy body is hard to grasp. In one region, it is said to have been born from hardened mud.

Name: Whiscash
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

Name: Galarian Yamask
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask’s dark power.

Name: Galarian Runerigus
Effects: For each damage counter on this Galarian Runerigus, put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

Name: Binacle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

Name: Barbaracle
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

Name: Carkol
Flavor-Text: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

Name: Coalossal
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy card, a [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Sandaconda V
Effects: Attach a [F] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Falinks
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has “Falinks” in its name.
Flavor-Text: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

Name: Falinks V
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have “Falinks” in their name take 20 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stonjourner
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

Name: Koffing
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Galarian Weezing
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Neutralizing Gas.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: From its rear, it sprays a foul-smelling liquid at opponents. It aims for their faces, and it can hit them from over 16 feet away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: In its belly, it reserves stinky fluid that it shoots from its tail during battle. As this Pokémon’s diet varies, so does the stench of its fluid.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Discard the top card of each player’s deck.
Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if a Stadium is in play, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

Name: Vullaby
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It wears a bone to protect its rear. It often squabbles with others of its kind over particularly comfy bones.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Although it’s a bit of a ruffian, this Pokémon will take lost Vullaby under its wing and care for them till they’re ready to leave the nest.

Name: Malamar V
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Malamar VMAX
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Impidimp
Flavor-Text: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

Name: Morgrem
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

Name: Galarian Meowth
Ability-Effect: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Galarian Perrserker, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon’s body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

Name: Scizor
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Scyther during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Once it has identified something as an enemy, it will continue beating them with its steel-hard pincers until there’s nothing left but scraps.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: It appears in ancient ruins. The pattern on its body doesn’t come from any culture in the Galar region, so it remains shrouded in mystery.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some believe it to be a deity that summons rain clouds. When angered, it lets out a warning cry that rings out like the tolling of a bell.

Name: Probopass
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.

Name: Durant
Effects: For each Energy attached to this Pokémon, search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: Honedge’s soul once belonged to a person who was killed a long time ago by the sword that makes up Honedge’s body.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Honedge evolves into twins. The two blades rub together to emit a metallic sound that unnerves opponents.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t apply more than 1 Big Shield Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: In this defensive stance, Aegislash uses its steel body and a force field of spectral power to reduce the damage of any attack.

Name: Copperajah V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Copperajah VMAX
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: Attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

Name: Zacian
Effects: Attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

Name: Zamazenta
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master’s Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Chatot
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Pidove
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon with [F] Resistance, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you’re scattering food for them, be careful—several hundred of them can gather at once.

Name: Tranquill
Flavor-Text: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

Name: Unfezant
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Discard up to 6 cards from the top of your deck. If you do, this attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It’s a huge help during tunnel construction.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Flavor-Text: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

Name: Stufful
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

Name: Bewear
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard your opponent’s Active Pokémon and all attached cards.
Flavor-Text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

Name: Skwovet
Flavor-Text: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

Name: Greedent
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

Name: Dubwool V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Boss’s Orders
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Burning Scarf
Description: If the [R] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if it is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Capacious Bucket
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 [W] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Cursed Shovel
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Dan
Description: Draw 2 cards. You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors until someone wins. If you win, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Full Heal
Description: Your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Galar Mine
Description: The Retreat Cost of both Active Pokémon is [C][C] more.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Milo
Description: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand, and draw 2 cards for each card you discarded in this way.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nugget
Description: You can play this card only if you drew it from your deck at the beginning of your turn, before you put it into your hand.Draw 3 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Oleana
Description: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Your opponent reveals their hand. Put a Trainer card you find there on the bottom of their deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Scoop Up Net
Description: Put 1 of your Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX into your hand. (Discard all attached cards.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Skyla
Description: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sonia
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tool Scrapper
Description: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s) and discard them.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Training Court
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may put a basic Energy card from their discard pile into their hand.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Turffield Stadium
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for an Evolution [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Capture Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to a Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Horror [P] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [P] Energy.If the [P] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

Name: Speed [L] Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [L] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to a [L] Pokémon, draw 2 cards.

Name: Twin Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX, it provides [C][C] Energy.If this card is attached to a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX, it provides [C] Energy instead.

Name: Rillaboom V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [G] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eldegoss V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ninetales V
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace V
Ability-Effect: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Milotic V
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Inteleon V
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent reveals their hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Boltund V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxtricity V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragapult V
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sandaconda V
Effects: Attach a [F] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.

Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does that much more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.

Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Frosmoth
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

Ability-Effect: Your [M] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
